skill.name: release-orchestrator
skill.description: Coordinate software release preparation, rollout readiness, migration notes, rollback planning, stakeholder communication, and final go/no-go release packets. Use when asked to prepare a release packet, review release readiness, or turn scattered launch notes into one reusable release workflow. Do not use for generic project planning, one-off announcement drafting, or high-level release theory.
skill.summary: Coordinate software release preparation, rollout readiness, migration notes, rollback planning, stak...
skill.author: yaojingang

security_audit.summary: Static high weak-cryptography detections are false positives; the affected lines are release descriptions, examples, and checklist text, not cryptographic code. Static external-command detections are Markdown code fences or inline file references, and no prompt injection, network exfiltration, or hidden execution intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Match
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography alerts point to ordinary release text and examples. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic usage at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain release-management descriptions and checklist headings, not cryptographic APIs or algorithms. The context confirms the static pattern matched unrelated wording.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Formatting
low_findings.description: The static Ruby or shell backtick alerts are Markdown formatting for templates, file paths, and decision labels. No evidence found of shell execution, command interpolation, or user-controlled command invocation at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged backticks delimit Markdown code spans and a fenced template. They do not form executable Ruby, shell, or dynamic command syntax.

content.value_statement: Release teams often collect rollout steps, migration notes, rollback triggers, and stakeholder messages in separate places. This skill turns those inputs into one structured release packet with go/no-go guidance.
actual_capabilities: Builds a structured release packet from scattered launch notes.
Checks for required release sections, including migration and rollback coverage.
Classifies release risk using low, medium, high, and critical severity guidance.
Produces stakeholder communication blocks for engineering, support, operations, and leadership.
Returns an explicit GO, GO WITH CONDITIONS, or NO-GO recommendation.
Flags missing release-critical inputs instead of inventing readiness details.
limitations: Requires accurate release inputs from the user or repository context.
Does not deploy software, run migrations, or approve production changes.
Does not replace human ownership for incident, compliance, or executive approval.
May need customization for organization-specific release gates.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Launch Review
use_cases.description: Convert release notes into a packet that reviewers can scan before approval.

use_cases.title: Validate Rollback Readiness
use_cases.description: Check whether a service release has concrete rollback triggers and recovery expectations.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Stakeholder Updates
use_cases.description: Create audience-specific communication blocks for engineering, support, operations, and leadership.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Packet
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with release notes already available.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use $release-orchestrator to prepare a release packet from these notes. Include scope, risks, rollout, rollback, communications, and a final decision.

prompt_templates.title: Review Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request before a release meeting.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use $release-orchestrator to review this release for readiness. Identify missing migration details, unclear rollback triggers, and any blockers.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Conditional Go Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a risky production launch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use $release-orchestrator to create a GO WITH CONDITIONS packet. Include owners, monitoring gates, rollout sequence, rollback criteria, and stakeholder messages.

prompt_templates.title: Standardize a Release Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request to make a reusable process.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use $release-orchestrator to turn these scattered launch notes into a reusable release workflow with decision thresholds and repeatable packet sections.

output_examples.input: A search release includes an index migration, staged traffic shift, latency monitoring, and support notifications.
output_examples.output: A release packet with scope, high migration risk, staged rollout steps, rollback triggers, audience-specific messages, and a GO WITH CONDITIONS decision.

output_examples.input: A release request mentions schema changes but does not provide migration timing or rollback criteria.
output_examples.output: A NO-GO packet that lists missing migration details, missing rollback triggers, and the release-critical questions needed before approval.

output_examples.input: A low-risk internal release needs a concise review packet for engineering leadership.
output_examples.output: A compact packet with release summary, low-risk assessment, no migration requirement, simple rollout checklist, rollback plan, and a GO recommendation.

best_practices: Provide migration details, rollout sequence, rollback triggers, owners, and monitoring signals together.
Ask for blockers explicitly when release data is incomplete or uncertain.
Review stakeholder messages before sending them outside the release team.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to approve a release without human accountability.
Do not provide vague rollback statements such as revert if needed.
Do not use it for a single announcement when no release packet is needed.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a structured release packet with scope, risks, migrations, rollout, rollback, stakeholder communication, and a final decision.

faq.question: Can it make a final production approval?
faq.answer: No. It can recommend GO, GO WITH CONDITIONS, or NO-GO, but accountable owners must approve production changes.

faq.question: Does it run deployment commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed for release planning and packet assembly, not live deployment execution.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Release scope, risky changes, migrations, rollout steps, rollback triggers, owner names, monitoring signals, and communication audiences work best.

faq.question: Can it handle migrations?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a migration section and flags missing migration details when schema, data, or configuration changes are present.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: It is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatibility.
